text: Each evening Confucius recommended that his students ask three questions of themselves: Have I been faithful in my dealings? Have I been sincere with friends? Have I mastered what I was taught? This ritual — called ri san xing wu shen, daily triple self-examination — was not moral bookkeeping but attentional training. By naming each domain explicitly, the practitioner directed focused review rather than vague rumination. Psychologists now call this implementation intention: specific, pre-committed mental acts that make desired behaviors far more likely to occur. The Confucian evening audit anticipated this mechanism by two and a half millennia.
question: According to the passage, what did Confucius's daily three-question practice primarily train?

options.text: Memory through repetition of the day's events
